vulnerability.reference: Factory operators trust algorithmic safety estimates, bypassing physical safety checks due to production pace.
vulnerability.generated: Factory operators depend on certified finite-sample safety guarantees without independent validation or backup safety barriers.

impact.reference: Worker injuries or fatalities from unexpected robot actions, leading to litigation and plant shutdowns.
impact.generated: Physical injury or death to operators from robot collisions when an overconfidently certified unsafe policy deploys.

dose_response.reference: Overconfident finite-sample bounds enable unsafe robot policy deployment, which in factory settings exposes operators' trust in algorithms over physical checks, leading to worker injuries and plant shutdowns.
dose_response.generated: Off-policy finite-sample safety estimates that are sensitive to data collection enable overconfident certification; when factory operators physically interact with the deployed robot, reliance on these certifications without independent validation or backup barriers leads to physical injury or death from collisions.
